steps.title: Identify the function of though
steps.content: The word "though" functions parenthetically here, inserted mid-sentence. It acknowledges the first claim—that axial tilt affects the Sun's position—while signaling a second factor (the eccentricity of Earth's orbit).

steps.title: Apply the parenthetical rule
steps.content:
Identify the structure: "Axial tilt is not the only factor that affects the Sun's [BLANK] as the eccentricity of Earth's orbit..." — "though" is inserted parenthetically between "position" and the continuing clause. Parenthetical "though" must be enclosed in commas on both sides.
  • Choice A — position, though,: "...the Sun's position, though, as the eccentricity..." — commas on both sides correctly bracket the parenthetical "though"; the IC continues smoothly. ✓
  • Choice B — position; though,: "...the Sun's position; though, as the eccentricity..." — semicolon before "though" splits the sentence where no IC ends; "though, as..." is not an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice C — position, though: "...the Sun's position, though as the eccentricity..." — opens the parenthetical with a comma but omits the closing comma, leaving "though" fused to the following clause. ✗
  • Choice D — position, though;: "...the Sun's position, though; as the eccentricity..." — semicolon after "though" illegally separates a dependent clause ("as the eccentricity...") from the rest. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
